name: Red Fort
description: A historic Mughal fort with expansive courtyards and museums.

name: Qutub Minar
description: Tall minaret and historical complex with Indo-Islamic architecture.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: Bustling old-market lane famous for street food and markets.

name: Jama Masjid
description: One of India's largest mosques with grand courtyards.

name: Humayun's Tomb
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and architectural precursor to the Taj Mahal.

name: Gateway of India
description: Iconic waterfront arch and popular ferry departures.

name: Marine Drive Promenade
description: Sunset promenade lined with street-food stalls and cafes.

name: Colaba Causeway
description: Shopping street with cafes, antique shops, and local eateries.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: Ancient rock-cut cave temples with stunning Shiva sculptures on an island.

name: Baga Beach
description: Lively beach known for water sports and nightlife.

name: Dudhsagar Falls
description: Powerful multi-tiered waterfall on the Goa-Karnataka border.

name: Fort Aguada
description: 17th-century Portuguese fort overlooking the Arabian Sea.

name: Calangute Beach
description: Goa's largest and most popular beach with shacks, water sports, and vibrant crowds.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: Distinctive pink sandstone palace with ornate windows.

name: Amer Fort
description: Hilltop fort with palaces, courtyards, and panoramic views.

name: City Palace Jaipur
description: Royal complex of palaces, courtyards, and museums in the heart of Jaipur.

name: Jantar Mantar
description: UNESCO-listed astronomical observatory with giant stone instruments.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
description: Serene canals and houseboat cruises through palm-fringed lagoons.

name: Spice Trail Plantation
description: Guided spice plantation walks with tasting sessions.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Scenic tiger reserve with elephant sightings and boat safaris on Periyar Lake.

name: Munnar Tea Gardens
description: Rolling hills blanketed with lush tea estates and misty valleys.

name: Hadimba Temple
description: Ancient wooden temple set in tranquil cedar forest.

name: Rohtang Pass
description: High-altitude mountain pass offering snow activities and breathtaking Himalayan views.

name: Kullu River Rafting
description: White-water rafting and river-side camping experiences on the Beas River.

name: Solang Valley
description: Popular ski resort valley with paragliding, zorbing, and snow activities.

name: Taj Mahal
description: World-famous white marble mausoleum and UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Agra Fort
description: Massive 16th-century Mughal fort and UNESCO site with palaces and mosques.

name: Itmad-ud-Daulah (Baby Taj)
description: Fine marble tomb often described as a draft of the Taj Mahal.

name: Mehtab Bagh
description: Moonlit garden across the Yamuna offering the best sunset view of the Taj Mahal.

name: Marudamalai Temple
description: Ancient hilltop temple with panoramic views of Coimbatore city.

name: Black Thunder Theme Park
description: Amusement park with water rides, roller coasters, and family attractions.

name: VOC Park & Zoo
description: Green park with walking trails, gardens, and a small zoo.

name: Dhyanalinga Temple
description: Serene meditation temple and spiritual centre by the Isha Foundation.

name: Marina Beach
description: India's longest beach with golden sands, vibrant atmosphere, and street food.

name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple
description: Iconic 7th-century Dravidian temple with stunning architecture and artwork.

name: Government Museum
description: One of India's oldest museums with ancient bronze sculptures and artifacts.

name: Elliot's Beach
description: Calm, scenic beach ideal for relaxed walks and picnics away from the crowds.

name: Fort St. George
description: India's first British fort, housing a museum of colonial-era artifacts.

name: Tiruchendur Murugan Temple
description: One of the six abodes of Lord Murugan, built into seaside cliffs with stunning ocean views.

name: Thoothukudi Seashore
description: Peaceful coastal stretch known for pearl fishing heritage and scenic sunsets.

name: Vivekananda Memorial Rock
description: Iconic memorial on a rocky island at India's southernmost tip where three seas meet.

name: Charminar
description: Iconic 16th-century mosque and monument at the heart of the old city.

name: Golconda Fort
description: Impressive medieval fort complex with acoustic wonders and panoramic views.

name: Hussain Sagar Lake
description: Large heart-shaped lake with a giant Buddha statue on a central island.

name: Victoria Memorial
description: Magnificent white marble colonial monument set in sprawling manicured gardens.

name: Howrah Bridge
description: Iconic cantilever bridge over the Hooghly River and symbol of Kolkata.

name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
description: Famous riverside Hindu temple associated with the mystic Ramakrishna Paramahamsa.

name: Shaniwar Wada Fort
description: Historic Peshwa-era fortification with massive bastions and lush gardens.

name: Aga Khan Palace
description: Beautiful palace with Indo-Islamic arches, significant as a Gandhi memorial site.

name: Sinhagad Fort
description: Hilltop fort with a challenging trek, stunning valley views, and historical importance.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of Hinduism's most sacred temples, dedicated to Lord Shiva on the Ganges.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat
description: The main ghat on the Ganges, renowned for its spectacular nightly Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Sarnath
description: Sacred Buddhist site where the Buddha gave his first sermon, with ancient stupas and a museum.

name: Fort Kochi Beach
description: Charming waterfront with iconic Chinese fishing nets, colonial architecture, and fresh seafood.

name: Mattancherry Palace
description: 16th-century Dutch palace with rare Kerala murals depicting scenes from the Ramayana.

name: Jew Town Spice Market
description: Historic marketplace famous for spices, antiques, and colonial-era synagogue.

name: Cubbon Park
description: Expansive 300-acre green lung of the city with heritage buildings and walking trails.

name: Bangalore Palace
description: Tudor-style royal palace with wood-panelled interiors, paintings, and sprawling grounds.

name: ISKCON Temple Bangalore
description: One of the largest ISKCON temples globally with stunning architecture and serene atmosphere.
